meta.siteDescription: Recognize, explore and record your emotions through emotion cards, and let your emotions become your power. 65 emotion cards help you become aware of your inner feelings, write emotional stories, and promote mental health.

aboutEmotions.sourceName: Hong Kong Federation of Youth Groups | Healthy Space (Kowloon)

whatIsEmotion.title: What are emotions?
whatIsEmotion.content: Emotion in English is emotion, which represents the energy flowing in our body. When we are stimulated and trigger inner feelings, physical reactions, thoughts and actions, we are called emotions. For example, when someone walks into a back alley and encounters a vicious-looking dog (stimulus), he feels very frightened and worries that he will be bitten (inner feelings and thoughts), his heart beats faster and he trembles (physical reaction), and he finally decides to take a quick detour and leave (action).

sixBasicEmotions.title: 6 basic emotions
sixBasicEmotions.content: Naturalist Darwin mentioned that humans have six basic emotions, including happiness, sadness, fear, disgust, anger and surprise, which are instinctive emotions that help humans improve their chances of survival. Other complex emotions, such as excitement, grievance, inferiority, jealousy, guilt, loneliness, etc., are derived from a mixture of basic emotions and arise after learning and socialization.

goodOrBad.title: Emotions are neither good nor bad
goodOrBad.content: Most people are used to classifying emotions into positive and negative. They feel that happiness is a positive emotion, while sadness, anger, and fear are harmful to people. In fact, emotions are neither good nor bad. Each emotion has its own unique meaning. For example, feeling fearful and uneasy about unfamiliar environments reflects the desire for security and reminds us to stay away from danger. Being vulnerable and crying when you are sad can attract care and support and promote interpersonal connections.

healthyEmotion.title: Emotional health
healthyEmotion.content: If you are biased against emotions, deny and suppress your true feelings, pretend to be happy when you are sad, or endure silently when you are angry, it will easily lead to physical and mental health problems. The so-called emotional health does not mean to be in a happy mood all the time, but to be able to be aware of your different emotions, understand your inner needs, and express and adjust them in a healthy and appropriate way to restore peace of mind.

guide.title: How to browse mood cards
guide.usageByView: Instructions for use of each interface
guide.switchViewDescription: Click the "Collapse" or "Expand" button on the upper right to change the display mode of the emotion card.
guide.tableDescription: Click the "Emotion Chart" on the upper right to quickly browse all emotion words.

expand.title: Expand
expand.description: Click on a single "Emotion Card" to view details about that emotion.

collapse.title: close
collapse.description: There are 9 category cards in total. After clicking on a single "category card", you can enter the emotion card page of that category.

table.title: emotion meter
table.description: Click on a single "emotion word" to view detailed content about that emotion.

guide.hint: 💬 You can collapse this description by clicking on the block titled "How to browse mood cards".

overview.categoryCardsHint: 🔍 There are 9 category cards below. Click on a category card to enter the emotion card page of that category.

category.emptyState: There are currently no mood cards

intro.line1: Are you lost in the world of emotions?
intro.line2: It doesn't matter, let's explore our emotions together in the wave emotion card.

breathing.line1: I invite you to close your eyes first,

awareness.line1: During the process of inhaling and exhaling,
awareness.highlight: Slowly feel the state of your body and mind

guide.title: Explore steps and methods
guide.categoryCardsHint: 🔍 There are 9 category cards below. Click on a category card to enter the emotion card page of that category.
steps: Scroll down the page to start browsing mood cards.
Click the "+" button on the upper right side of the emotion card to put the emotion card into "My Emotion Card Holder".
After completing the selection of emotion cards, click the "My Emotion Card Holder" button to go directly to the next step.
browseMethods: Click the "Collapse" or "Expand" button on the upper right to change the display mode of the emotion card.
Click the "Emotion Chart" on the upper right to quickly browse all emotion words.

errors.tooManyDescriptionLine1: The maximum number of cards is 3.
errors.tooManyDescriptionLine2: Please filter for the cards that best match your emotions.

cards.emptyState: There are currently no mood cards

strength1.title: The intensity of my emotions | First time

instructions.line1: Thank you for your efforts in finding your own emotional card.
instructions.line2: Next, I want to invite you to close your eyes again, take a deep breath and exhale~
instructions.line3: During the process of taking a deep breath, feel yourself in your heart and what is the intensity of the selected emotions?
instructions.line4: You can remove a card with the "X" in the top-right corner. If you want to add another card, go back to the previous step.
instructions.scoreHint: (1 point represents the weakest, 5 points represents the strongest)

errors.tooManyDescriptionLine1: The maximum number of cards is 3.
errors.tooManyDescriptionLine2: Please filter for the cards that best match your emotions.
errors.levelsDescription: The emotional intensity of each emotion card must be filled in.

strength2.title: The intensity of my emotions | The second time

instructions.line1: After noticing and recording emotional stories, I wonder if your current emotions are okay?
instructions.line2: I invite you to take a deep breath again and ask yourself, what is the intensity of your emotions now?
instructions.scoreHint: (1 point represents the weakest, 5 points represents the strongest)
instructions.skipHint: If you don't want to record it, you can skip this step and click "Save Record" directly.

instructions.line2: Next, I would like to invite you to slowly dive into your own emotional story and find the source of your emotions.
instructions.skipHint: If you don't want to record it yet, you can skip this step and click "Next" directly.

prompt.title: I invite you to gently close your eyes, and between inhaling and exhaling, slowly recall what happened before you felt the emotion "{cardNames}"?
prompt.description: In the column below, please try to write down the reasons, things, background stories, etc. that you think may have caused you to have these emotions.

intro.line1: Thank you for trying hard to recall the background and reasons for the emotion.
intro.line2: Next, I want to invite you to think back together after the emotional wave comes...
intro.skipHint: If you don't want to record it yet, you can skip this step and click "Next" directly.

question1.label: Question 1
question1.prompt: How did you react after feeling the emotion of "{cardNames}"? Or what did you do?
question1.example: For example: After feeling the anxiety in my heart, I wrote down various things I was worried about in my diary.

question2.label: Question 2
question2.prompt: What was the result of your reaction above?

question3.label: Question 3
question3.prompt: How do you feel about the above results?

question4.label: Question 4
question4.prompt: Do you think the result of this is what you expected?

question5.label: Question 5
question5.promptUnexpected: If the results this time are not what you expected, what do you think you can do next time you have similar emotions to get the results you expected?
question5.promptAffirming: In this emotional journey, what do you think you did well? You are invited to write down what you are sure you did well.

complete.title: Thank you for being with the emotional wave
complete.saveSuccess: The record has been saved successfully.
complete.thanks: Thank you for spending time, thought, awareness, recording and reflection on yourself.
complete.selfTalkIntro: I want to invite you to say to yourself:
complete.selfTalkFull: I am willing to be aware of and embrace my emotions and myself. This way I am already great!
complete.selfTalkLine1: I am willing to be aware of and embrace my emotions and myself
complete.hint: You can click "Done" in the upper right corner and go to "My Records" to view the record content.

fields.nextActionAndAffirmationLine2: And what I want to affirm about myself

ratio.title: Emotional group ratio
ratio.description1: The picture below is a pie chart of the proportion of times the emotion cards you recorded during this period appeared in the following categories.
ratio.description2: If you want to see the proportion of that category, you can hover the cursor over the color block of that category in the pie chart.
